alerts.title: Iran threatens to abandon US-Iran framework deal if Israeli strikes on Lebanon continue
alerts.summary: Iran's Foreign Ministry stated that continued Israeli military strikes on Beirut could derail the US-Iran framework MoU scheduled for Friday signing in Switzerland, marking the first explicit linkage of ceasefire agreement viability to Israeli military operations (incidents feed: Axios, Al Jazeera, Iran media outlets). Threat level remains at 9 (CRITICAL—IMMINENT ESCALATION per global_metrics).

alerts.title: Israel strikes Beirut's Dahieh; Trump publicly rebukes Netanyahu over judgment
alerts.summary: Israeli Defense Force conducted strikes in Beirut's southern suburbs (Dahieh) on June 15, killing at least 1-3 civilians, with ongoing strikes despite Trump's explicit public statement that Israeli actions jeopardize the US-Iran peace agreement and that Netanyahu lacks sound judgment (NBC News, Reuters, Axios incidents feed). Public US-Israel fracture over de-escalation strategy unprecedented in current negotiation cycle.

alerts.title: Iran nuclear program remains at 60% enrichment; breakout window 1-2 weeks amid deal talks
alerts.summary: Iran maintains uranium enrichment at 60% U-235 with estimated 1-2 week breakout capability while framework agreement proceeds toward signing (global_metrics). IAEA board resolution demanding inspection access and uranium stock reporting unresolved, indicating nuclear verification remains major contentious issue despite diplomatic progress.

alerts.title: Iran-linked cyber group claims breach of FBI drone systems; threatens World Cup targeting
alerts.summary: Iran-linked threat actor reportedly claimed successful hack of US FBI drone control systems and threatened targeting of World Cup 2026 event in retaliation for earlier Iranian strikes (global_metrics cyber_headlines: France 24, CBS News, Middle East Eye). Multiple reports suggest potential operational capability; cyber-as-escalation dimension emerging parallel to kinetic operations.

alerts.title: Three US carriers and ten combat vessels positioned across Middle East theater; USS Florida submarine in Persian Gulf
alerts.summary: US military maintains heavy concentration: USS Dwight D. Eisenhower (Carrier, Red Sea), USS Abraham Lincoln (Carrier, Arabian Sea), USS Gerald R. Ford (Carrier, Mediterranean), plus five destroyers/amphibious ships (USS Carney, Mason, Laboon in Red Sea; USS Bataan, USS Boxer in Pacific theater), and USS Florida submarine (Deployed—Persian Gulf per assets feed). Deployment posture consistent with high threat-level status and contingency positioning ahead of framework deal signing.
alerts.region: Strait of Hormuz, Red Sea, Arabian Sea

analysis_note: US-Iran framework agreement hangs on edge as Israeli military operations risk deal collapse; nuclear enrichment remains unresolved; Iran escalates cyber operations; US military posture remains at high readiness across contested zones.
